text:
Market today: Mood is cautiously risk-on. The real driver is China's stronger factory activity tied to tech export demand — that's a direct tailwind for semiconductors and the names you're heavy in. Iran deal removing an oil shock is also helping sentiment broadly.

Your holdings: SOXL (-7.06%) is the one demanding attention — it's a 3x leveraged chip ETF that decays daily, and you're already underwater. Stop is $200 but a bad week cuts there fast on margin. SMH (-0.69%) is fine for now but watch it as the unleveraged read on chip health. SPCX is brand-new, very volatile IPO with almost no price history — tiny moves can gap through stops; keep size small and manage it manually, margin makes that gap hurt double.

Watch: 1) SMH price action — it's your canary for whether today's China tailwind actually flows into chip stocks. 2) Oil staying above $70 — energy cost pressure matters for data center capex, which feeds your whole sector.

Bottom line: SOXL is your biggest live risk — know exactly what you're doing with that stop before the day gets away from you.
